title: Type and colour, from an empty file
checkLabels.t2: The file is no longer empty
checkLabels.t7: The font list has a generic name
checkLabels.t10: The prices use a monospace font
why.untouched: styles.css still holds only the comment. The whole file is yours.
hint2.untouched: Start at the top: the variables, then body, then the blocks.
why.unlinked: The page cannot find styles.css. You changed the link line.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and start over.
why.used-inline: You used style in the tag. That is lesson 01 again.
why.touched-html: You edited the HTML. Here you only write styles.
why.no-hero-h1: There is no heading in the hero block.
why.no-price: There is no element with the price class.
why.no-muted: There is no element with the muted class.
why.no-btn: There is no element with the btn class.
why.no-vars: The colours are not pulled into variables.
hint2.no-vars: You will need the brown in at least three places. Declare it once at the top.
why.no-generic: The font list has no generic name at the end.
hint2.no-generic: The last name should be a kind, not a specific font — it is the only one that cannot be missing.
hint2.no-leading: One and a half times the font size. With no unit, so it is inherited as a ratio.
why.heading-loose: The heading lines are too far apart.
hint2.heading-loose: A large font needs less air. Between 1.1 and 1.3.
why.price-not-mono: The prices do not use a monospace font.
hint2.price-not-mono: Numbers in a column read more easily when every character is the same width.
why.muted-contrast: The note does not have enough contrast.
hint2.muted-contrast: It should be quieter than the text and still pass 4.5 to 1 on white.
why.btn-inline: The button does not accept sizes.
hint2.btn-inline: A link is inline. It needs inline-block.
why.btn-underlined: The button is still underlined like a link.
hint2.btn-underlined: The button shape and background already say it is clickable. The underline is redundant here.
blocks.0.text: Sixteen lessons about text and colour. The page opposite is bare: the HTML is there, styles.css is empty. This time it is not enough that it works — it has to read.
blocks.2.items.0: The colours live in variables at the top, not scattered through the file.
blocks.2.items.1: The font has fallbacks and ends with a generic name.
blocks.2.items.2: The body text breathes and the headings are tighter than it.
blocks.2.items.3: The prices use a monospace font and are right-aligned so the numbers read.
blocks.2.items.4: The note is quieter than the text but passes the contrast threshold.
blocks.2.items.5: The link at the bottom looks and behaves like a button.
blocks.4.text: Variables first — they are the decisions. Then body: font, size, line height, colour. Then the headings. The special cases last: prices, note, button. Start from the button and you will paint it a colour you then have to change in three places.
blocks.5.text: The numbers and colours are yours. The check looks at whether the text reads, not at whether you picked that exact brown.
blocks.7.text: Write styles.css from scratch. Do not touch the HTML. There is no single right answer; there are eight things that have to behave correctly.
